channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Hamas agrees to return bodies of 4 Israeli hostages in exchange for hundreds of Palestinian prisoners
item.description: Hamas has agreed to return the bodies of more dead Israeli hostages with just days to go before the end of the first phase of the ceasefire agreement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israelis bid farewell to a mother and her two young sons killed in captivity in Gaza
item.description: Tens of thousands of Israelis have lined highways holding flags, orange balloons and signs saying “forgive us” in an outpouring of grief as the bodies of a mother and her two small sons who were killed in captivity in the Gaza Strip were taken for burial.
item.dc:creator: Melanie Lidman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Analysts back Trump’s USAID cuts in Africa, say increased trade will better benefit continent's poor
item.description: Some analysts believe that President Donald Trump was right to cut aid to Africa and that increased trade with the continent will fill "the plates of hungry kids at the end of the day.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Hamas to turn over bodies of 4 Israeli hostages in exchange for release of hundreds of prisoners
item.description: CAIRO (AP) — Hamas will return the bodies of four dead Israeli hostages on Thursday in exchange for Israel's release of hundreds of Palestinian prisoners, the group said, just days before the first phase of the ceasefire between the warring parties was to expire.
item.dc:creator: SAMY MAGDY and MELANIE LIDMAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: South Korea's Yoon defends his martial law decree as impeachment ruling nears
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — In a final statement at his impeachment trial, South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol defended his martial law decree that plunged the country into chaos as a bid to inform the public of the danger of the opposition-controlled parliament as he vowed Tuesday to push for politi
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Funeral held for Shiri Bibas and her sons after their remains handed over by Hamas
item.description: A funeral procession for a mother and her two young sons whose remains were handed over by Hamas is being held Wednesday morning in Rishon Lezion, Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Latvia FM: Putin will try to achieve in peace talks what he couldn't in Ukraine war
item.description: Latvia’s foreign minister is warning that Russian President Vladimir Putin will try to achieve in peace talks what he hasn’t been able to accomplish in his war against Ukraine — weaken the United States and restore control over his smaller neighbor.
item.dc:creator: Edith M. Lederer, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Power outage leaves most of Chile in darkness for hours
item.description: SANTIAGO, Chile (AP) — Electricity providers across Chile scrambled to restore service Tuesday after a sweeping blackout plunged 90% of the country into darkness, stranding commuters, knocking cell service offline and paralyzing businesses and daily life.
item.dc:creator: NAYARA BATSCHKE and MATIAS BASUALDO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel and Hamas agree on new exchange, leaving fragile ceasefire intact
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israeli and Hamas officials said Tuesday they have reached an agreement to exchange the bodies of dead hostages for the release of hundreds of Palestinian prisoners, keeping their fragile ceasefire intact for at least a few more days.
item.dc:creator: JOSEF FEDERMAN and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Argentina's Milei skirts Congress to seat a divisive judge in the Supreme Court
item.description: BUENOS AIRES, Argentina (AP) — Argentina's President Javier Milei temporarily appointed two Supreme Court judges by decree Tuesday, bypassing Congress during its summer recess to push through a particularly controversial candidate in a move criticized as an overreach of executive power.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Botswana, the country with the biggest diamonds, gets an improved 10-year deal with miner De Beers
item.description: GABORONE, Botswana (AP) — The Botswana government signed a new diamond sales agreement crucial to its economy with the De Beers mining company on Tuesday after seven years of negotiations.
item.dc:creator: SELLO MOTSETA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: At least four dead in South Korea highway construction project collapse
item.description: At least four people were killed and six others were injured after concrete structures supporting a bridge collapsed at a highway construction site in South Korea.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: New search to begin for Malaysia Airlines Flight 370: Reports
item.description: The latest search comes after the Malaysian government in December agreed to accept a second "no find, no fee" proposal from the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: Syria's national dialogue conference held as the battered country seeks to rebuild
item.description: DAMASCUS, Syria (AP) — Syria’s new rulers held a long-awaited national dialogue conference Tuesday, which they touted as as a “rare historical opportunity” to rebuild the country after the fall of former President Bashar Assad and nearly 14 years of civil war.
item.dc:creator: OMAR ALBAM and ABBY SEWELL, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Power outage in Chile leaves millions without electricity as officials scramble for answers
item.description: A widespread power outage in the South American nation of Chile left millions of people without electricity Tuesday, according to news reports.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Former Hamas hostage briefs UN Security Council on the 'pure hell' that was captivity in Gaza
item.description: Former Hamas hostage Noa Argamani spoke to the U.N. Security Council on Tuesday about her time as a captive in Gaza. She also made a desperate plea for the release of the remaining hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelensky expected to visit White House amid push for minerals deal
item.description: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky is expected to visit the White House on Friday as Kyiv and the Trump administration work to finalize an agreement on critical minerals.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Trump says minerals deal has been 'pretty much' negotiated with Zelenskyy, meeting slated for Friday
item.description: President Donald Trump and Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy have agreed to the terms of a minerals deal, allowing U.S. access to Ukrainian natural resources.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Vatican says Pope Francis is critical but stable with no new respiratory crises
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis was in critical but stable condition Tuesday as he worked from the hospital while battling double pneumonia, and the Vatican announced some major governing decisions that suggest he is getting essential work done and looking ahead.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: NATO nation Poland scrambles air defenses as Russia strikes western Ukraine
item.description: Poland once again launched air defenses after Russia fired long-range strikes across Ukraine, hitting near the shared border with Poland just hours after leaders met in Kyiv to pledge more support.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Bill O'Reilly: Trump wants Nobel Peace Prize via Ukraine peace deal
item.description: O'Reilly defended Trump's approach to ending the Russia-Ukraine conflict, which has entered its third year.
item.dc:creator: Leland Vittert, The Hill

item.title: Pope Francis shows slight improvement and resumes some work, while still critical, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis remained in critical condition Monday but showed slight improvement in laboratory tests and resumed some work, the Vatican said, including calling a parish in Gaza City that he has kept in touch with since the war there began.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD and TRISHA THOMAS, Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Australian treasurer seeking tariff exemption in talks with Bessent
item.description: Australia’s treasurer, Jim Chalmers, will continue to push for steel and aluminum tariff exemptions in his discussions with Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent this week.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, The Hill

item.title: The Latest: Ukraine marks third anniversary of war as US shifts its policy under Trump
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A dozen leaders from Europe and Canada are in Ukraine to mark the third anniversary of Russia’s invasion and discuss support with President Volodymyr Zelenskyy. They include European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen and Canadian Prime Minister Justin Trudeau. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: These 17 countries voted with US against Ukraine UN resolution
item.description: Seventeen countries in the United Nations General Assembly joined the U.S. on Monday in voting against a nonbinding resolution condemning Russia as the aggressor in the ongoing war in Ukraine — more than double the vote against a similar resolution two years ago.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Crisp, The Hill
